title: quadlet ignores DNS settings
body:
### Issue Description

When I use quatlet and define DNS either in network or pod or container (I testet IPv4 and IPv6) it is ignored.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set DNS=10.90.0.2 in pod or container service file
2. Start pod
3.


### Describe the results you received

dns:/# cat /etc/resolv.conf
search localdoamin
nameserver 2xxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx::1
nameserver 10.90.0.1

These are the IP addresses of the podman1 network, not the DNS I have specified to use.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect that I have the option to configure DNS so that this nameserver appears in /etc/reslov.conf of the container.
Is this expectation correct or not?

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

### Additional information


```
[root@bat bind]# cat /etc/containers//systemd/dns.pod
[Unit]
Description=dns
After=local-fs.target

[Pod]
PodName=dns
DNS=2xxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx::2
DNSSearch=localdoamin
Network=podman1
IP=10.90.0.2
IP6=2xxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx::2

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

```
[root@bat bind]# cat /etc/containers//systemd/bind.container

[Unit]
Description=bind

[Container]
Pod=dns.pod
DNS=2xxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx::2
Image=localhost/bind
Volume=bind_db:/var/bind
Volume=bind_conf:/etc/bind
Volume=bind_pki:/etc/pki

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

comments.body:
This is expected and not related to quadlet. networks that have dns_enabled set to true (i.e. all new bridge networks by default unless --disable-dns is used) will use aarvavark-dns and therefore will have the aardvark-dns ip in resolv.conf.
That does not mean --dns is ignored, the dns is server is simply passed to aaravark-dns instead and it will forward to these server for the specific container requests.

I have disabled DNS in my network and set the network in my pod.

Now /etc/resolv.conf in the container contains the DNS server I have configured for the pod.
